issue_title: JGroups bind port configuration ignored when --cache-embedded-network-bind-port set
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

Configuring a custom bind port with `--cache-embedded-network-bind-port` does not change the port.

### Version

26.6.0-SNAPSHOT

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The bind port should be configured as expected when `--cache-embedded-network-bind-port` is explicitly configured.

### Actual behavior

The default bind port is used.

### How to Reproduce?

Configure `--cache-embedded-network-bind-port=7801` and observe that the logs show 7800 port:

```
INFO  [org.infinispan.CLUSTER] (main) ISPN000079: Channel `ISPN` local address is `fedora-54374`, physical addresses are `[127.0.0.2:7800]`
```

### Anything else?

Can be workaround by configuring the bind port using the system property `-Djgroups.bind.port=7801`.

The root cause is that the `JGroupsConfigurator.SystemProperties` sets the JGroups property `-Djgroups.bind_port` not the Infinispan property `jgroups.bind.port` and so the default value of `7800` that is used in Infinispan's default stack is used.

The `jgroups.bind.port` does not exist in JGroups, therefore if a user provides their own JGroups stack file this property  won't take effect. To cover both scenarios, we can set both the `jgroups.bind.port` and `jgroups.bind_port`  property in the `JGroupsConfigurator`.
commit_message:
JGroups bind port configuration ignored when --cache-embedded-network-bind-port set

Closes #46663

Signed-off-by: Ryan Emerson <remerson@ibm.com>

issue_title: IntegrationTest sub class @TestSetup called before super class
commit_message:
Fix @TestSetup method ordering with inheritance (#46686)

* Fix @TestSetup method ordering with inheritance

ReflectionUtils.listMethods() had two bugs when class inheritance was
involved:

1. Subclass methods were added before superclass methods, resulting in
   wrong execution order. Expected behavior is superclass first, matching
   standard Java inheritance semantics.

2. When a subclass overrides a superclass method annotated with
   @TestSetup, both methods were collected causing the subclass method
   to execute twice instead of only the override running once.

The fix builds the class hierarchy top-down (superclass first) and skips
superclass methods that are overridden by a subclass method with the
same name and parameter types.

Closes #46667

Signed-off-by: hammadxcm <hammadkhanxcm@gmail.com>

* Small refactoring to add when not already added, instead of adding/remove

Signed-off-by: stianst <stianst@gmail.com>

issue_title: Raise minimum maximum number of `poolMaxSize` as value 3 shown in Keycloak example leads to acquisition timeout after switch to JDBC Ping
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

operator

### Describe the bug

When you follow documentation example (which should not be taken literally) in https://www.keycloak.org/operator/advanced-configuration#_server_configuration_details you have db config like:

```yaml
spec:
  db:
    poolInitialSize: 1
    poolMinSize: 2
    poolMaxSize: 3
```

However, after `cache-stack` default switch from K8 to JDBC PING in #40201 (cc @ryanemerson ), having 3 connection may not be enough on startup as there seem to be nested transactions in `runJobInTransaction`, for example:

```
JGroupsConfigurator.configureDiscovery (line 251)                                                 
    ↓ starts TRANSACTION 1 via KeycloakModelUtils.runJobInTransactionWithResult                     
      ↓ calls prepareJGroupsAddress (line 270)                                                      
        ↓ calls getNextSequence (line 297)                                                          
          ↓ starts TRANSACTION 2 (NESTED!) via KeycloakModelUtils.runJobInTransactionWithResult
            ↓ tries to access database via JpaServerConfigStorageProvider.loadOrCreate
              ↓ Hibernate tries to acquire connection...  --> leads to timeout
```

And thus, application startup fails. 

### Version

26.4.7+ (that is what I verified, probably 26.3.x+, give it a try)

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

At the very least, we need to adjust the documentation example. But raising minimal value of the `poolMaxSize` would be highly desirable and we need to test that our new minimum works as expected.

### Actual behavior

Application fails to start. I can see following stacktrace:

commit_message:
feat(quarkus): validate and doc min poolMaxSize is 4+ for JDBC PING (#46737)

Currently when migration tasks are run, we see that the app startup fails with DB pool size 3 or less for JDBC_PING.
Hence, we started to validate that the minimal max pool size is 4 for JDBC PING.

Closes: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/46673

Signed-off-by: Michal Vavřík <michal.vavrik@aol.com>

issue_title: Group-level deny policies do not block `manage-group-membership` on group members
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/fine-grained-permissions

### Describe the bug

In fine-grained admin permissions, the `manage-group-membership` scope on Users has no scope alias mapping to the Groups resource type. Because of this, deny policies on a specific group do not block `manage-group-membership` for that group's members, even though the same pattern works correctly for other scope pairs like `manage` / `manage-members` and `view` / `view-members`.

### Version

main (latest)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A deny policy on a specific group with `manage-membership` scope should also block `manage-group-membership` on users who belong to that group, the same way denying `manage-members` on a group blocks `manage` on its members.


### Actual behavior

The group-level deny has no effect on `manage-group-membership`. An admin with `manage-group-membership` granted on All Users can still add or remove protected group members to and from other groups.


### How to Reproduce?

1. Enable Admin Permissions in Realm Settings
2. Create a group `vault` and add a protected user to it
3. Create a positive User policy for an accounts admin
4. Create a negative User policy for the same accounts admin
5. Create a Users permission granting `view`, `manage`, `manage-group-membership` on All Users with the positive policy
6. Create a Groups permission granting `view-members`, `manage-members`, `manage-membership` on All Groups with the positive policy
7. Create a Groups permission denying `view-members`, `manage-members`, `manage-membership` on the `vault` group with the negative policy
8. As the accounts admin, attempt to add a vault member to a different group — it succeeds when it should be denied


### Anything else?

The root cause is in [`AdminPermissionsSchema.java` line 106](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/33ff9f1b71e1c6e10c54bce4d72b4cc69d9d81f6/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/fgap/AdminPermissionsSchema.java#L106). The Users resource type scope alias map defines:

```java
Map.of(VIEW, Set.of(VIEW_MEMBERS), MANAGE, Set.of(MANAGE_MEMBERS), IMPERSONATE, Set.of(IMPERSONATE_MEMBERS))
```

`MANAGE_GROUP_MEMBERSHIP` has no alias to `MANAGE_MEMBERSHIP`, so `FGAPEvaluation.isDenied()` returns `false` (empty aliases at [line 101](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/33ff9f1b71e1c6e10c54bce4d72b4cc69d9d81f6/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/fgap/evaluation/FGAPEvaluation.java#L101)) when evaluating group-level deny policies against this scope.

The fix is adding the missing alias:

```java
Map.of(VIEW, Set.of(VIEW_MEMBERS), MANAGE, Set.of(MANAGE_MEMBERS), IMPERSONATE, Set.of(IMPERSONATE_MEMBERS), MANAGE_GROUP_MEMBERSHIP, Set.of(MANAGE_MEMBERSHIP))
```
commit_message:
Fix FGAP deny evaluation for manage-group-membership

Add the missing Users FGAP scope alias from manage-group-membership to Groups manage-membership so deny permissions on group members apply during user membership updates.

Add a regression test covering a protected group member, an unrelated user, and the no-mutation postcondition after a forbidden request.

Closes keycloak#46693

issue_title: UMA permission grant accepts expired ID token claim_token and issues RPT
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

This was shared with the Keycloak team via its Bug Bounty. 

We'd like to thank Evan Hendra (Independent Security Researcher) for reporting this in issue YWH-PGM40475-94

## Summary
- UMA Permission Grant accepts an **expired OIDC ID Token** as `claim_token` and still issues an RPT (HTTP 200), bypassing token expiration.

## Vulnerability Type
- Authentication Bypass

## Affected Component(s)
- Keycloak Server — UMA Permission Grant

## Keycloak Configuration Context (If Applicable)
- Confidential client `uma-client` (directAccessGrantsEnabled=true) 
- Resource server client `uma-rs` (authorizationServicesEnabled=true, serviceAccountsEnabled=true) 
- Authorization Services configured:
  - Scope: `read`
  - Resource: `res1` with scope `read`
  - User policy: `allow-uma-user` for `uma_user`
  - Scope permission: `perm-res1`
- Realm: `master`

## Impact
Expired ID Tokens can still be used to obtain a fresh RPT, enabling access to UMA‑protected resources beyond intended expiry or after logout. This breaks authentication
## Recommendations
- Enforce `exp-`, Reject expired ID Tokens at UMA permission grant.

### Version

26.5.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Expired ID tokens should be rejected

### Actual behavior

Expired ID tokens are accepted. They are only rejected when the user session has ended. 

### How to Reproduce?

Shared with the Keycloak team internally.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: UnsupportedOperationException in V1 Token Exchange Audience Validation (FGAPv2 Incompatibility)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

token-exchange

### Describe the bug

Thank you for binreaper for submitting this to our bug bounty program in YWH-PGM40475-143

## Summary

Keycloak 26.5.3 crashes with `UnsupportedOperationException` when performing a V1 token exchange with an `audience` parameter targeting a different client. The `V1TokenExchangeProvider.validateAudience()` calls `AdminPermissions.management().clients().canExchangeTo()`, which dispatches to `ClientPermissionsV2` when FGAPv2 is enabled (default in 26.5.3). `ClientPermissionsV2.canExchangeTo()` throws `UnsupportedOperationException("Not supported in V2")` instead of performing the authorization check, resulting in an HTTP 500 error.

## Vulnerability Type

Denial of Service / Improper Error Handling (CWE-754: Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions)

## Affected Component(s)

- `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/fgap/ClientPermissionsV2.java` — line 142-143
- Called from: `V1TokenExchangeProvider.validateAudience()` — line 254

## Keycloak Configuration Context

- TOKEN_EXCHANGE feature enabled (`--features=preview` or `--features=token-exchange`)
- FGAPv2 (Fine-Grained Admin Permissions V2) enabled — this is `Type.DEFAULT` in 26.5.3, so it applies to ALL default installations
- Any realm with at least two clients



### Version

26.5.3 

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Do not log exceptions in the server log. 

Suggestions from the hunter: 

**Option 1** (preferred): Implement `canExchangeTo()` in `ClientPermissionsV2` using the V2 permission evaluation framework.

**Option 2**: Graceful fallback — return `false` (deny) instead of throwing:
```java
@Override
public boolean canExchangeTo(ClientModel authorizedClient, ClientModel to, AccessToken token) {
    return false; // V2 does not yet support client exchange permissions
}
```

**Option 3**: Guard in `V1TokenExchangeProvider` with try-catch around the `canExchangeTo()` call, returning HTTP 403 on `UnsupportedOperationException`.

### Actual behavior

500 response code, and an exception in the server log. 

### How to Reproduce?

## Steps to Reproduce (PoC)

1. Start Keycloak 26.5.3 with token exchange enabled:
```
docker run -p 8080:8080 -e KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME=admin -e KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin \
  quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:26.5.3 start-dev --features=preview
```

2. Create a realm "test" with a user "alice" (password: alice123) and ensure at least two clients exist (admin-cli and broker are present by default).

3. Get a user token:
```bash
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8080/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/token \
  -d "grant_type=password&client_id=admin-cli&username=alice&password=alice123" \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['access_token'])")
```

4. Attempt token exchange with a different audience:
```bash
curl -v -X POST http://localhost:8080/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/token \
  -d "grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange" \
  -d "client_id=admin-cli" \
  -d "subject_token=$TOKEN" \
  -d "subject_token_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:access_token" \
  -d "audience=broker"
```

5. **Expected**: HTTP 403 (exchange not permitted) or HTTP 200 (exchange succeeded)
   **Actual**: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error:
```json
{"error":"unknown_error","error_description":"For more on this error consult the server log."}
```

6. Server logs show unhandled exception:
```
ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-1)
Uncaught server error: java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException: Not supported in V2
    at org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.fgap.ClientPermissionsV2.canExchangeTo(ClientPermissionsV2.java:143)
    at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.tokenexchange.V1TokenExchangeProvider.validateAudience(V1TokenExchangeProvider.java:254)
    at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.tokenexchange.AbstractTokenExchangeProvider.exchangeClientToClient(AbstractTokenExchangeProvider.java:227)
```



### Anything else?

## Detailed Description

Three classes interact to cause the crash:

1. **`AdminPermissions.management()`** (line 57-62): When `ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ_V2` is enabled (which is `Type.DEFAULT` in 26.5.3), returns `MgmtPermissionsV2`.

2. **`MgmtPermissionsV2.clients()`** (line 87-91): Returns `ClientPermissionsV2`.

3. **`ClientPermissionsV2.canExchangeTo()`** (line 142-143): Throws `UnsupportedOperationException("Not supported in V2")` — the V2 implementation was never completed for this method.

The V1 token exchange code at `V1TokenExchangeProvider.validateAudience()` line 254 calls:
```java
AdminPermissions.management(session, realm).clients().canExchangeTo(client, targetClient, token)
```

This chain resolves to `ClientPermissionsV2.canExchangeTo()` which unconditionally throws.

Note: Self-exchange (audience = same client) works because the code short-circuits before reaching `canExchangeTo()`. Only cross-client audience exchange triggers the crash.

Also affects `exchangeToPermission()` and `mapRolesPermission()` in the same class — same `throw new UnsupportedOperationException("Not supported in V2")` in three methods (lines 142-148, 152-153).

## Impact

1. **Broken OAuth feature**: V1 token exchange with `audience` parameter is completely non-functional on any default Keycloak 26.5.3 installation with token exchange enabled. This is a documented OAuth feature (RFC 8693) that applications may depend on for service-to-service token delegation.

2. **Authorization check bypassed by crash**: The `canExchangeTo()` method is supposed to perform an authorization check (allow/deny). Instead of returning true/false, it crashes. The permission system fails before making any decision.

3. **Server-side log pollution**: Each request generates a full Java stack trace, which can be used for log flooding.
commit_message:
Fix deny unsupported FGAPv2 token-exchange audience checks

* Deny unsupported FGAPv2 token-exchange audience checks

When FGAPv2 is enabled, ClientPermissionsV2.canExchangeTo() was throwing UnsupportedOperationException, which caused V1 token exchange audience validation to fail with HTTP 500. Return false instead so unsupported checks are denied gracefully.

Closes #46718

Signed-off-by: Oluwatobi Mustapha <oluwatobimustapha539@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: Alexander Schwartz <alexander.schwartz@ibm.com>
Co-authored-by: Alexander Schwartz <alexander.schwartz@ibm.com>

issue_title: NullPointerException Crash in SessionCodeChecks When Client is Disabled During Authentication Flow
issue_body:
When an administrator disables a client while a user has an active authentication session, submitting the login form causes an unhandled `NullPointerException` in `SessionCodeChecks.initialVerify()`. This crash occurs because the method correctly detects the disabled client but then attempts to call `this.clientCode.removeExpiredClientSession()` before `clientCode` has been initialized. While this triggers a localized server error, the crash occurs before authentication processing, meaning there is no token issuance, no data leakage, and no bypass of security controls.

## Version affected

Keycloak 26.1.4 and Keycloak 26.5.4.

## Expected behavior

The server should gracefully handle the disabled client state and return a proper error page to the user, rather than crashing with an unhandled exception.

## Actual behavior

The server crashes with an unhandled `NullPointerException` on the authentication endpoint. Users see a generic HTTP 500 error instead of a helpful message, and the error is logged as an `Uncaught server error` in the server logs.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Start Keycloak in dev mode and obtain an admin access token.
    
2. Create a test realm and an OIDC client with implicit flow enabled.
    
3. Create a test user with credentials.
    
4. Start the authentication flow to obtain the login form URL.
    
5. Disable the client via the admin API while the user's login session is still active.
    
6. Submit the credentials to the login form.
    
7. Observe the HTTP 500 status and the `NullPointerException` in the server logs.

issue_title: ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException in ArtifactBindingUtils when SAMLart parameter is too short
issue_body:
### Description



## Description

When a SAML artifact binding request is received at `/realms/{realm}/protocol/saml` with a `SAMLart` parameter that decodes to fewer than 2 bytes, `ArtifactBindingUtils.byteArrayToResolverProviderId()` reads `ar[0]` and `ar[1]` without first checking the length of the array. This causes an unhandled `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException`, resulting in an HTTP 500 response.

## Version affected

26.5.4 (latest stable)

## Expected behavior

A malformed or undersized `SAMLart` value should be rejected early with an appropriate client error (HTTP 400) before any byte-level parsing is attempted.

## Actual behavior

Submitting `SAMLart=YQ==` (valid Base64 decoding to a single byte) causes Keycloak to throw an `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException` in `ArtifactBindingUtils.byteArrayToResolverProviderId()` and return HTTP 500. No authentication is required to trigger this.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Start Keycloak 26.5.4 in production mode with SAML artifact binding enabled.
2. Send the following request (no authentication required):

```
POST /realms/master/protocol/saml
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

SAMLart=YQ==
```

3. Observe HTTP 500 response and `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException` in server logs.

## Relevant code

- `server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/saml/util/ArtifactBindingUtils.java` line 10 and 14 — array read at index 0 and 1 without prior length validation.

## Suggested fix

Add a length check in `byteArrayToResolverProviderId()` before accessing `ar[0]` and `ar[1]`, and return an explicit error or throw an `IllegalArgumentException` if the input is shorter than the minimum expected artifact length (which per the SAML artifact spec should be at least 4 bytes for the type code and endpoint index).
commit_message:
Add length check in byteArrayToResolverProviderId to prevent out of bounds exception

see original

issue_title: Authentication Failure with Mixed-Case Email Domain in Organizations
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue[/+]
- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.[/+]

### Area

organizations

## Describe The Bug
Users attempting to log in to Keycloak Organizations with an email containing mixed-case domain characters (e.g., `User@MixedCase.com`) intermittently receive an error page. Checking the logs reveals a `NullPointerException` (NPE) in `InfinispanOrganizationProvider.getByDomainName`.

The issue starts working after organizational changes (like deleting and recreating an organization).

## Why?
The root cause is a mismatch between administrative operations and the authentication cache:
1. **Case-Sensitive Cache**: The Infinispan cache uses the raw domain string as a key. A login with `MixedCase.com` creates a cache entry distinct from `mixedcase.com`.
2. When an organization is updated or deleted, Keycloak only invalidates the *lowercase* version of the domain in the cache.
3. **Stale Cache Entry**: If a mixed-case entry exists (`MixedCase.com`), it remains in the cache even if the organization is deleted.
4. When a subsequent login uses the mixed-case domain, Keycloak hits the stale cache entry, finds an organization ID that no longer exists in the DB, and throws an NPE when trying to process that null result.



### Version

26.5.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Logging in with a mixed-case email domain (e.g., `user@Domain.Com`) should work identical to lowercase. 
If an organization is deleted or updated, all related cache entries should be cleared immediately. 
If a cache entry is stale, Keycloak should gracefully fall back to the database instead of crashing.

### Actual behavior

Logging in with a mixed-case domain creates a separate, case-sensitive cache key. 
When an organization is deleted, Keycloak only invalidates the lowercase version of the domain. Subsequent logins using the mixed-case domain hit a stale cache entry, attempt to map a deleted organization ID, and throw a NullPointerException, resulting in an authentication failure page for the user.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create an organization with domain `test.com`.
2. Log in with `user@Test.Com` (creates a mixed-case cache entry).
3. Delete and recreate the organization (invalidates `test.com`, but leaves `Test.Com` in cache).
4. Attempt to log in again with `user@Test.Com` -> NPE / Auth failure.
commit_message:
Fix NPE on mixed-case email domain in Keycloak Organization

This commit addresses a NullPointerException that occurs when logging in with a mixed-case email domain in a Keycloak Organization. The root cause was a mismatch between how the JPA layer (case-insensitive) and the Infinispan cache layer (case-sensitive) handled domain name casing.

Key changes:
- Normalized the domain name to lowercase when generating Infinispan cache keys in InfinispanOrganizationProvider.
- Added defensive null checks when resolving organizations from the cache to prevent NullPointerException from stale cache entries.
- Added a new integration test testStaleCacheEntryDoesNotThrowNPE in OrganizationCacheTest to verify the fix.
- Fixed a flaky test (incorporated @martin-kanis commit)

Signed-off-by: asafm <asafm1989@gmail.com>

commit_message:
Validate .conf extension for --config-file option (#47696)

* Validate .conf extension for --config-file option

The --config-file option and KC_CONFIG_FILE environment variable
silently ignored configuration files that did not use the .conf
extension. This happened because the underlying SmallRye config
source loader filters by file extension, returning an empty list
for non-.conf files without any indication to the user.

Added explicit validation in both the CLI option setter and the
config source file resolution to reject files that do not end
with .conf, giving the user a clear error message instead of
silently ignoring their configuration.

Closes #46978

Signed-off-by: Tanner Smith <griffonbyte@proton.me>

* fix: validate config file extension before checking existence

Move the .conf extension check before the file existence check in
setConfigFile(). When a file with a wrong extension does not exist
relative to the working directory, the existence check would fire
first, masking the more specific extension validation error.

Closes keycloak/keycloak#47696

issue_title: createCurlContainer method implicitly depends on DockerHub
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

Inside the testsuite's K8sUtils.java, the createCurlContainer method creates a new pod, referencing a particular image coordinate (`curlimages/curl:8.1.2`) that does not specify an image registry. This, by deprecated convention, implicitly means to use DockerHub as the registry for the image. There are two problems with this:

1. Kubernetes systems that have fully deprecated the old convention (such as OpenShift Container Platform 4.21) now refuse to accept image coordinates that do not specify a registry.
2. DockerHub frequently applies rate limiting that can cause test failures for no reason.

Replacing this image coordinate with `quay.io/curl/curl:8.1.2` would resolve these problems, while providing the same image that the tests depend on.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Test cases relying on the createCurlContainer method should work on OCP 4.21

### Actual behavior

Test cases relying on the createCurlContainer method fail to start due to OCP refusing to accept an unqualified image coordinate.

### How to Reproduce?

Run the testsuite with OCP 4.21

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Use quay.io instead of DockerHub in testsuite createCurlContainer()

Closes #47025

Signed-off-by: Alex Szczuczko <aszczucz@redhat.com>

issue_title: Account ResourceService user endpoint returns excessive user data in UMA-enabled realms
issue_body:
## Description

The Account REST API endpoint `GET /realms/{realm}/account/resources/{ownedResourceId}/user?value={usernameOrEmail}` returns the full `UserRepresentation` object when resolving users for resource sharing in UMA-enabled realms.

The endpoint currently uses `toRepresentation()` which includes fields beyond what the resource sharing UI requires:

- `totp`
- `requiredActions`
- `disableableCredentialTypes`
- `attributes`

Additionally, the endpoint returns HTTP 200 for existing users and HTTP 204 for non-existent users.

The root cause is in `ResourceService.java` where the full representation is returned instead of a minimal DTO appropriate for the resource sharing UI context.

## Version affected

Latest stable release

## Expected behavior

The endpoint should:

1. Return only the minimal user information necessary for the sharing UI (id, username, firstName, lastName, email)
2. Return consistent response structures regardless of whether a user is found

## Actual behavior

The endpoint returns the complete `UserRepresentation` including all fields, and differentiates between existing and non-existing users via HTTP status codes.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Enable User-Managed Access (UMA) on a realm
2. Create two users with custom attributes configured
3. As the first user, create or own any resource
4. Issue a GET request to `/realms/{realm}/account/resources/{ownedResourceId}/user?value={secondUsername}`
5. Observe the response contains the full UserRepresentation
commit_message:
Account ResourceService user endpoint returns excessive user data in UMA-enabled realms

Closes #47036

issue_title: [OID4VCI] Credential definition must not contain `@context` when not using JSON-LD
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

Credential metadata contains invalid `credential_definition`

```
    "oid4vc_natural_person_jwt": {
      "format": "jwt_vc_json",
      "scope": "oid4vc_natural_person_jwt",
      "credential_definition": {
        "type": [
          "oid4vc_natural_person"
        ],
        "@context": [
          "oid4vc_natural_person"
        ]
      },
    },
```

Only for format `ldp_vc` the spec allows ...

```
      "credential_definition": {
        "@context": [
          "https://www.w3.org/2018/credentials/v1",
          "https://www.w3.org/2018/credentials/examples/v1"
        ],
        "type": [
          "VerifiableCredential",
          "UniversityDegreeCredential"
        ]
      },
```


### Version

latest

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

No `@context` for format `jwt_vc_json`
commit_message:
Exclude @context from credential definition for non-JSON-LD formats

Only include @context in credential_definition when the credential
format is ldp_vc, as required by the OID4VCI specification. For
jwt_vc_json format, @context is now excluded by setting it to null
in JwtCredentialBuilder. The LDCredentialBuilder now explicitly
contributes credential definition metadata including @context.

Closes #47045

Signed-off-by: Asish Kumar <officialasishkumar@gmail.com>

issue_title: Duplicated flows can have illegal characters in step name
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area admin/ui ### Describe the bug When you duplicate an authentication flow, all the steps are prefixed with the new flow name. Aside from the fact that that breaks it (if you use 'check if flow is executed'), it can create steps with illegal characters. If you use `(` or `/` for instance, you end up with this: It says: ``` Could not save the config: character '/' not allowed. ``` ### Version 26.5.4 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior I'm protected from using characters that break things later on. Or, when renaming all the steps, don't use illegal characters. However, the best expected behavior is that the step names aren't required to be globally unique, so that they don't have to be renamed, and therefore conditions that refer to step names don't break upon duplication. But, don't know if that's too hard. ### Actual behavior See first post. ### How to Reproduce? * Duplicate a flow and name it `my duplicate (breaking chars)`. * Try to edit steps and save them. ### Anything else? _No response_

issue_title: NPE when regenerating client secret when client policy with client-updater-context exists
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When a Client Policy using condition `client-updater-context` exists, trying to regenerate a client secret from admin UI will throw a NPE.

See stacktrace:
[stacktrace.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25906895/scratch_68.json)

### Version

26.5.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

No NPE is thrown

### Actual behavior

NPE is thrown `Cannot invoke "org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.AdminAuth.getToken()" because "this.adminAuth" is null`

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create and enable Client Policy using `client-updater-context` condition
2. Click "Regenerate" at Clients -> client-name -> Credentials (this throws an error and you can check server logs for details)

We are running this on a realm separate from "master", but I don't think this makes a difference.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix NPE in ClientSecretRotationContext when client policy with client-updater-context exists

ClientSecretRotationContext was passing null for the AdminAuth parameter
to its parent constructor. When a client policy with the
client-updater-context condition is active, the condition evaluates
context.getToken() which delegates to adminAuth.getToken(), causing a
NullPointerException because adminAuth is null.

Pass the AdminAuth instance from the admin endpoint through to the
context constructor, consistent with how AdminClientUpdateContext and
other admin CRUD contexts are constructed elsewhere in ClientResource.

Closes #47063

Signed-off-by: easonysliu <easonysliu@tencent.com>

issue_title: CVE-2026-3429 Improper Access Control for LoA During Credential Deletion
issue_body:
## Description

There is an improper access control vulnerability in the Keycloak Server Account API. When an authentication flow is configured with Step-up Authentication and subflows conditioned by Level of Assurance (LoA) , the system fails to enforce these LoA boundaries during credential deletion. Specifically, a user logged in with a lower LoA can issue a `DELETE` request to remove credentials associated with a higher LoA. This allows an attacker who has compromised a user's password to delete the user's registered MFA (e.g., OTP) and potentially register a new MFA device to escalate their own session's LoA.

## Version affected

26.5.1

## Expected behavior

The Account API should validate the current session's LoA against the required LoA for the credential being modified or deleted. A session authenticated at a lower LoA (e.g., password only) should be denied permission to delete a credential that enforces a higher LoA (e.g., OTP).

## Actual behavior

The endpoint `/realms/[realmName]/account/credentials/[credentialld]` allows the deletion of any of the user's credentials, regardless of the session's current LoA. A user authenticated only via password (LoA 1) can successfully delete an OTP credential (LoA 2).

## Steps to reproduce

1. Configure a realm with ACR to LoA mappings (e.g., `1->1`, `2->2`).
    
2. Create a browser authentication flow that requires a Password for LoA 1 and an OTP for LoA 2.
    
3. Configure a client to use this step-up browser flow.
    
4. Create a test user, log in at LoA 2, and register an OTP.
    
5. Authenticate as the test user requesting only LoA 1 (password only) to obtain an access token.
    
6. Using this LoA 1 access token, send a `GET` request to `/realms/{realm_name}/account/credentials` to obtain the ID of the OTP credential.
    
7. Send a `DELETE` request to `/realms/{realm_name}/account/credentials/{credential_id}` using the LoA 1 access token.
    
8. The OTP credential is successfully deleted despite the session not having LoA 2 privileges.

---
*This issue was originally tracked in the private repository. Migrated by @abstractj.*
commit_message:
CVE-2026-3429 Improper Access Control for LoA During Credential Deletion for the case of client overriden flow
closes #47069

Signed-off-by: mposolda <mposolda@gmail.com>

issue_title: Do not allow fetching organizations of a member if not a member of the current organization
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

When calling `/organizations/{org}/members/{user}/organizations`  there is no check to make sure the user referenced by `{user}` is a member of the given {org}.

This was reported by `snejad123` via the security mailing list and classified to not be a security issue as information is only revealed to an admin. 

### Version

26.5.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Only allow returning organizations of a member if the user is member of the current org.

### Actual behavior

When calling `/organizations/{org}/members/{user}/organizations`  there is no check to make sure the user referenced by `{user}` is a member of the given {org}.

### How to Reproduce?

* Create two orgs
* Create two users and make each of them a member of different orgs
* Call `/organizations/{org}/members/{user}/organizations` using a user that is not a member of the org

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Enforce org membership on member organizations endpoint

Signed-off-by: Oluwatobi Mustapha <oluwatobimustapha539@gmail.com>

issue_title: Do not allow managing invitations if not an invitation of the current organization
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

When calling /organizations/{org}/invitations/{id} there is no check to make sure the invitation referenced by {id} belongs to the organization referenced by `{org}`.

Thank you for `uzet`  to report this via the security mailing list. This was triaged not to be a security problem as the data is still revealed only to the realm admin who can manage all invitations.

### Version

26.5.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Only allow returning invitations if they belong to the current org.

### Actual behavior

When calling /organizations/{org}/invitations/{id} there is no check to make sure the invitation referenced by {id} belongs to the organization referenced by `{org}`.





### How to Reproduce?

* Create two orgs
* Create invitations for each org
* Call `/organizations/{org}/invitations/{id}/organizations` using invitation ids and orgs that are isolated from each other.


### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Do not allow managing invitations if not an invitation of the current organization

Closes #47080

Signed-off-by: vramik <vramik@redhat.com>
